skill.name: lark-im
skill.description: Lark/Feishu IM integration: Send and receive messages, manage group chats, search message history, and handle file resources through the lark-cli tool.
skill.summary: Comprehensive Lark/Feishu instant messaging integration for sending messages, managing chats, searching history, and handling file resources.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Lark/Feishu IM integration skill using the lark-cli tool. Static analysis flagged 1397 patterns (external_commands: 1121, filesystem: 68, network: 9), but evaluation confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' are documented CLI usage examples in markdown, 'path traversal' references are relative documentation links, and 'crypto' flags are API token references. The skill requires explicit user confirmation before sending messages and follows proper authentication protocols.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI Tool
medium_findings.description: Skill uses lark-cli binary for all operations. This is declared in metadata requires.bins and is expected for CLI-based skills. Commands are structured with hardcoded arguments, not user input injection. Risk mitigated by CLI tool being pre-approved and commands being template-based.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: CLI usage is documented and structured - commands follow predictable patterns with no dynamic user input injection vectors detected

low_findings.title: File Download Capability
low_findings.description: Skill can download files from Lark messages to local filesystem. Output paths are validated to prevent traversal attacks (.. not allowed). User must explicitly specify output path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File download feature explicitly documented with path validation rules preventing traversal attacks

low_findings.title: API Endpoint References
low_findings.description: Documentation contains hardcoded Lark/Feishu API URLs. These are expected for API integration skills and point to official endpoints only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: API URLs are documented endpoints for the official Lark/Feishu platform - standard for integration skills

content.user_title: Send messages and manage Lark/Feishu chats
content.value_statement: Integrate Claude with Lark/Feishu messaging to send messages, manage group chats, search conversation history, and handle file resources. Automate team communication workflows with secure bot or user identity authentication.
actual_capabilities: Send text, markdown, post, image, file, video, and audio messages to Lark/Feishu chats
Create, update, and manage group chats with user and bot members
Search messages across chats with keyword, sender, and time range filters
List messages in chats and threads with pagination support
Download images and files from messages to local storage
Add reactions to messages and manage emoji responses
Pin messages and manage chat member lists
limitations: Requires lark-cli tool to be installed and authenticated with Lark/Feishu
Bot identity operations require the bot to be added to target chats
User identity operations require explicit user authentication via access tokens
Cannot send messages without explicit user confirmation of recipient and content

use_cases.title: Team Communication Automation
use_cases.description: Automate sending project updates, status reports, or alerts to team group chats. Schedule regular messages or trigger notifications based on external events.

use_cases.title: Customer Support Integration
use_cases.description: Connect Claude to Lark support channels to respond to customer inquiries, search conversation history for context, and escalate issues by pinging team members.

use_cases.title: Personal Productivity Assistant
use_cases.description: Use Claude to search your message history, download shared files, organize chat resources, and draft responses for review before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Send a text message
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a message to the team group chat saying "Meeting starts in 10 minutes in Conference Room A"

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Search message history
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my messages for any files shared by [user_name] in the last week about the project roadmap

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Create and configure a group chat
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new private group called "Q2 Planning" with the following team members: [list open_ids], set the description to "Quarterly planning discussions", and make the bot a group manager

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Download and organize chat resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all images shared in the [chat_name] chat this month, download them to ./downloads/ with organized filenames, and create a summary list of what was downloaded

output_examples.input: Send a message to chat oc_123 saying hello
output_examples.output: Message sent successfully to chat oc_123 (Engineering Team). Message ID: om_abc123. The bot posted: "hello"

output_examples.input: Search for messages containing "budget" from last month
output_examples.output: Found 15 messages matching "budget" across 3 chats. Results include messages from Finance Team chat and Project Alpha chat, with dates ranging from Feb 1-28. Top result: spreadsheet attachment shared by Finance Director on Feb 15.

output_examples.input: Download the image from message om_xyz789
output_examples.output: Downloaded image img_v3_abc123 from message om_xyz789. Saved to: ./img_v3_abc123.png (2.4 MB). File type verified as PNG image.

best_practices: Always confirm message recipient and content with the user before sending any message
Use bot identity for automated notifications and user identity for personal assistant tasks
Verify chat membership before attempting operations - bots must be added to target chats
Handle pagination for large result sets using --page-limit or --page-all flags
Use dry-run mode to preview API calls before executing sensitive operations
anti_patterns: Never send messages on behalf of users without explicit confirmation of content and recipient
Do not guess user open_ids or chat_ids - always fetch them via the appropriate lookup commands first
Avoid sending raw markdown with complex formatting - use --content flag for precise control
Do not attempt to download files without user specifying a safe output location

faq.question: What authentication is required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need to authenticate with Lark/Feishu using lark-cli. Run lark-cli auth login first. Bot operations use tenant_access_token; user operations use user_access_token.

faq.question: Can this skill send messages as me or as a bot?
faq.answer: Both. Use --as user for personal messages (sent as you) or --as bot for automated messages (sent as the app bot). Bot identity requires the bot to be added to target chats.

faq.question: How do I find a chat_id or user open_id?
faq.answer: Use +chat-search to find groups by name or keyword. Use contact +get-user to look up a user's open_id. The skill cannot operate without these identifiers.

faq.question: Why can't the bot resolve sender names in fetched messages?
faq.answer: Bot visibility settings may not include the message senders. Check app visibility in Lark Developer Console or use --as user to fetch messages with broader contact access.

faq.question: Can this skill download files from any chat?
faq.answer: Only chats where the authenticated user or bot has access. The caller must be a chat member. Files must be under 100MB (Lark API limit).

faq.question: What message types does this skill support?
faq.answer: Text, markdown, post (rich formatted), image, file, video, audio, sticker, interactive cards, share_chat, share_user, and merged forward messages.
